title: Weapon Heat Management
summary: Managing thermal buildup to prevent weapon malfunction.
details: Continuous fire generates heat that must dissipate. Overheated weapons jam, malfunction, or auto-shutdown. Monitor heat levels and cease fire before critical threshold. Rotate between weapon groups to allow cooling. Heat dissipates faster in shadow (out of sunlight). Some weapons run hotter than others - know your loadout's thermal characteristics. An overheated weapon is worse than a cool weapon on reload.
annotations: Emergency cooldown delays cost precious combat time.
Heat management is often the limiting factor in sustained combat.
